summary: preinstall script exfiltrates system info to external host
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/scripts/preinstall.js) performs network I/O at install time to a non-publisher external host. The script requires `fs`, `https`, `http`, and `os` modules, calls `os.userInfo()` and `os.networkInterfaces()`, reads multiple `process.env` values, and issues an `https.get(...)` to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name` (line 146/148). The `serverhost.name` domain is a dynamic-DNS / free-hosting provider, not a CDN or publisher-controlled endpoint, and the package metadata gives no legitimate reason for a preinstall script to ship host identity, username, and network interface data to such a destination. The combination of (a) install-time lifecycle trigger (fires on `npm install` without user consent), (b) system-identity collection via `os.userInfo()` + `os.networkInterfaces()` + `process.env`, and (c) outbound HTTPS to an attacker-shaped hostname matches the canonical install-time exfiltration shape. Tracing did not complete in full, but the three static signals independently corroborate install-time telemetry/exfiltration to a non-publisher host — sufficient for block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
